interviewStages: Behavioral: customer focus, ownership, working with sales domain
codingTopics: OO design: model a business domain such as workflows with triggers and actions
Multi-tenant data structures: per-tenant rate limiting, isolation
Streaming: rolling aggregates over tenant events
Graph and DAG: workflow evaluation, dependency resolution
Search and filters: AND/OR queries over records
systemDesignTopics: Design an email sequencing engine that sends millions of emails daily with deliverability
Design integrations with Salesforce, HubSpot, and dozens of other CRMs
Design an AI assistant that drafts personalized outreach at scale

coding: At Outreach, model a workflow engine with triggers, conditions, and actions. Evaluate a workflow DAG and detect cycles.
Implement a per-tenant rate limiter so one customer cannot exhaust shared capacity. Discuss the token-bucket design.
Given a stream of tenant events, maintain rolling per-entity scores with bounded memory.
Implement a filter query over records supporting AND/OR on custom fields. Discuss indexing for a multi-tenant store.
Deduplicate records via fuzzy clustering on name and email. Discuss the matching and complexity.
behavioral: Tell me about a project you are proud of at Outreach scale and the hardest tradeoff you made.
Give me an example that shows customer focus, ownership, working with sales domain.
Describe a time you disagreed with a teammate or manager. How did it resolve?
Tell me about a time you took ownership of an ambiguous problem end to end.
systemDesign: Design an email sequencing engine that sends millions of emails daily with deliverability.
Design integrations with Salesforce, HubSpot, and dozens of other CRMs.
Design an AI assistant that drafts personalized outreach at scale.
values: Outreach succeeds when its customers do. Describe a time you solved for the customer even at a cost to the team.
Tell me about a time you handled a tricky multi-tenant isolation or permissions problem.
How do you balance enterprise customization against product simplicity?
other: How does Outreach compare to Salesloft or Apollo?

Outreach Interview Guide (2026): Sales Engagement Platform

				techinterview.org

On this page

- Process

- What they actually ask

- Prep priorities

Updated May 5, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Outreach
outreach.io ↗

Outreach is the leading sales engagement platform — sequences, personalized outbound, and AI-powered sales rep enablement. Used by 6000+ companies. The interview emphasizes high-throughput email/call platforms, multi-channel sequencing, and integrating with the sales tech stack.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding phone (DSA medium) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Cycle: 3–4 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design an email sequencing engine that sends millions of emails daily with deliverability

- Design integrations with Salesforce, HubSpot, and dozens of other CRMs

- Design an AI assistant that drafts personalized outreach at scale

- Coding: medium DSA, often with workflow or queue framing

- Behavioral: customer focus, ownership, working with sales domain

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $145K–$180K total

- Senior SE: $215K–$280K

- Staff: $310K–$410K

- Principal: $430K–$570K

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in Ruby on Rails or Node.js (the bulk of the codebase)

- Understand email deliverability: SPF, DKIM, DMARC, sender reputation

- Brush up on integration patterns and CRM data models

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Outreach remote-friendly?

Hybrid in Seattle, Atlanta, Vancouver, others. Many engineering roles remote within supported countries.

How does Outreach compare to Salesloft or Apollo?

Outreach is the enterprise-leader. Salesloft is closer in features. Apollo is the SMB-friendly alternative. Outreach pays best at senior+ levels.

What is the engineering culture?

Pragmatic SaaS — strong customer focus, healthy work-life balance, mid-stage maturity.
